title: ranapalus rotundifolius
intro: Ranapalus rotundifolius, also known as Ranunculus rotundifolius, is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant is native to Western Australia, where it grows in sandy soils and produces yellow flowers. The leaves of the plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, joint pain, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream, and is often used in traditional remedies to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
